item.title: CVC nears deal for IFF’s $4bn food ingredients division
item.description: Private equity firms increasingly seek to carve out underperforming divisions of big corporate groups

item.title: Russian drone hits apartment building in Romania
item.description: Nato and Bucharest condemn ‘reckless’ escalation after two people were injured in Galaţi

item.title: Ocado strikes ecommerce deal with Asda
item.description: Partnership comes after British technology group suffered blow to US business

item.title: FirstFT: Russia overspends on Putin’s Ukraine war by $28bn
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Israel attacks Beirut and EU chip supply

item.title: FT Wealth: May
item.description: In this edition: The €1bn Ponzi scheme which rocked France; what football and finance have in common; the power of Christopher Anderson’s pictures; downsides to tax moves; Monaco’s many faces; London’s priciest sushi menu

item.title: Marc Rowan rolled in Hamptons lobster fight
item.description: A New York court ruling puts the Apollo chief back at ‘square one’ in a tussle with locals

item.title: Several BP shareholders found ousted chair ‘challenging’
item.description: Several large investors say it was hard to arrange meetings with Albert Manifold before his removal from oil major

item.title: Drone start-up Stark set for €2.5bn valuation in new fundraising
item.description: German company aiming to secure at least €300mn from investors

item.title: ‘Bonkers’: the audacious plan for a 1mn-person city near Cambridge
item.description: Shiv Malik, a former journalist, conceived his vision of timber skyscrapers after chronicling how young people were struggling

item.title: Not using AI in public services would mean ‘choosing decline’, UK minister warns
item.description: Newly appointed chief secretary to the Treasury Lucy Rigby wants to roll out technology across Whitehall

item.title: Investors race to get exposure to SpaceX ahead of IPO
item.description: Net $14bn pours into funds with stakes in rocket company while ETF providers line up wave of new products

item.title: Nigel Farage loses viral touch to Musk-backed Rupert Lowe
item.description: Far-right offshoot Restore Britain gains traction on social media platform X ahead of key Makerfield by-election

item.title: Urgent clarity on policy needed on the energy transition
item.description: The impact of Middle East war is a reminder why certainty is crucial to spur a shift from fossil fuels

item.title: Milei to privatise Argentina’s $10-a-night workers’ hotels
item.description: Libertarian government targets vast ‘social tourism’ complexes founded under Perón

item.title: If Alphaville was running the UK’s Debt Management Office . . .
item.description: Pulay for a day

item.title: Why Britain’s fintech stars faded
item.description: Curve, GoCardless, PrimaryBid and Railsr have all sold up since the start of last year

item.title: Are consumers ready for humanoid robots?
item.description: Should we be worried about the future?

item.title: KPMG Australia’s CEO resigns after whistleblower claims
item.description: Big Four firm says its investigations into allegations of data misuse lacked rigour

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during testing
item.description: Failure comes days before planned launch of internet satellites for Amazon

item.title: US to designate Brazilian drug cartels as terrorist groups
item.description: Move is likely to reignite tensions between Donald Trump and Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva

item.title: Disney says US regulator aims to ‘suppress speech’ with licence review
item.description: FCC has ordered the ABC owner to reapply for broadcast permissions after clash with Trump administration

item.title: UK and European passports linked to restricted Chinese investors
item.description: Critics raise concerns around critical national infrastructure and potential counterfeiting

item.title: Amazon scraps AI leaderboard to stop workers chasing usage scores
item.description: Senior executive Dave Treadwell tells staff ‘don’t use AI just for the sake of using AI’ as costs rise

item.title: The chip and memory stock frenzy
item.description: As software lags, semiconductors catch up to the AI spending

item.title: Musk’s tweet undermines SpaceX’s claims about Anthropic data centre deal
item.description: Billionaire says the arrangement, described in IPO filings as a three-year agreement, only lasts for 180 days

item.title: Chevron CEO warns oil prices to jump over summer as supplies dwindle
item.description: Blockade in Strait of Hormuz has removed up to 13mn barrels a day from global markets and drawn down ‘shock absorbers’

item.title: Anthropic finalises $65bn funding deal to surpass OpenAI’s valuation
item.description: New round values Claude AI maker at $965bn including the new money

item.title: AkzoNobel should avoid painting itself into a corner
item.description: Dutch paint maker’s original tie-up with Axalta doesn’t look especially compelling

item.title: Caesars’ billionaire buyer hopes luck and leverage go hand in hand
item.description: The transaction comes at a time when Caesars and Las Vegas have been struggling

item.title: And just like that, the software apocalypse was over
item.description: Snowflake’s stock pop demonstrates what companies must do to escape the sector downturn
